_comment: Three-tier least-privilege profiles for Copilot MCP tools. The active profile filters what's available from .vscode/mcp.json. Switch profiles via env var COPILOT_MCP_PROFILE.

read-only.description: Default for most developers and all chat modes that shouldn't mutate anything. Cannot write files, cannot run commands, cannot change infrastructure.

standard.description: Regular development work. Can edit workspace files, run local commands (tests, lint, build), open PRs. Cannot mutate infrastructure.

elevated.description: Platform / SRE engineers only. Can modify live infrastructure within narrow bounds. Still denies irrecoverable operations.

_notes.how_to_switch: Set COPILOT_MCP_PROFILE=standard before opening VS Code, or configure via .vscode/settings.local.json. The profile is read at VS Code startup — restart after changing.
_notes.enforcement: Profile filtering is applied by the MCP client in VS Code. It is NOT a security boundary against a compromised token — if an attacker has your GITHUB_TOKEN, they can call GitHub APIs directly outside Copilot. Pair profiles with token scoping, hook-level policy checks (.github/hooks/scripts/*), and least-privilege IAM.
_notes.auditing: All tool calls are logged in the Copilot audit log (Business/Enterprise). Review weekly for unexpected patterns.
